summary:
Carpal tunnel syndrome is a well-documented form of nerve entrapment caused by mechanical compression of the median nerve within the carpal tunnel. It is universally categorized as the most common peripheral nerve entrapment, supported by medical literature emphasizing compression pathology and targeted decompressive treatments. 

\boxed{A}
